stem: With a standard category B driving licence you may drive a passenger car up to:

choices.text: 3,500 kg with up to 8 passenger seats besides the driver
Any weight, as long as it has 4 wheels

explanation.short: Category B: passenger cars up to 3,500 kg with at most 8 passenger seats.
explanation.examTip: A trailer over 750 kg needs category BE.

stem: How long is a new category B driving licence normally valid?

explanation.short: A new B licence is normally valid for 10 years.
explanation.examTip: From age 75 a medical declaration is required at renewal.

stem: You passed your driving test 2 years ago. Which alcohol limit applies to you?

choices.text: No limit applies in your case

explanation.short: Beginner drivers face a 0.2‰ limit for the first 5 years after passing.
explanation.examTip: The beginner period is 7 years if you passed before age 18.

choices.text: That a car has a valid APK
Who is registered as responsible for the vehicle

explanation.short: The kentekenbewijs shows the registered keeper, who is liable for tax and offences.
explanation.examTip: Always transfer the registration when you sell the car.

stem: Which insurance is the legal minimum for a motor vehicle on the public road?

explanation.short: WA (third-party liability) is the legal minimum for any motor vehicle.
explanation.examTip: Without WA you may not even park the car on a public road.

stem: You will not use your car for several months. How can you legally pause the road tax?

choices.text: By suspending the registration (schorsen) at the RDW and parking the car off the public road
By covering the car with a tarpaulin on the street

explanation.short: Schorsen at the RDW pauses road tax, but the car must then stay off the public road.
explanation.examTip: Parking a suspended car on the street is itself an offence.

stem: What is the general legal blood alcohol limit for experienced drivers in the Netherlands?

explanation.short: The general limit is 0.5‰, or 0.22 mg/l in breath.
explanation.examTip: Beginner drivers have a stricter limit of 0.2‰.

stem: Your prescription medicine has a yellow sticker warning that it may affect driving. You should:

choices.text: Drive as normal — the sticker is only advisory
Check with the pharmacist or doctor before driving
Take a double dose so it wears off faster

explanation.short: Yellow-sticker medicines can impair driving — check before getting behind the wheel.
explanation.examTip: Driving while unfit is an offence even if the substance was prescribed.

stem: You are stopped by the police and refuse to take a breath test. What is the legal consequence?

choices.text: It is treated as if you had exceeded the alcohol limit

explanation.short: Refusing a breath test is punished as if you had exceeded the limit.
explanation.examTip: You can be required to give a blood sample as well.

stem: Most minor traffic offences in the Netherlands are dealt with under:

explanation.short: Minor offences are handled administratively under Wet Mulder.
explanation.examTip: Serious offences (drink-driving, dangerous driving) go through criminal court.

stem: After a minor collision with another car you have:

choices.text: No duty to stop if nobody is hurt
A duty to stop and exchange details with the other party
A duty only to call the police
A duty only to take a photo and drive on

explanation.short: You must always stop after an accident and exchange details.
explanation.examTip: Leaving the scene of an accident is a criminal offence.

stem: Both drivers sign the European accident form (schadeformulier) at the scene. By signing you:

choices.text: Confirm only the factual record of the accident
Waive your right to any insurance claim
Pay the other party on the spot

explanation.short: Signing confirms the facts only; liability is decided by the insurers.
explanation.examTip: Never leave parts blank — fill in or cross out every box.
